description: Unavailable on Cloudflare Workers. NetEase Mail requires IMAP/SMTP, so run this provider from the Node.js runtime.

fields.label: Email Address
fields.description: The full personal NetEase Mail address to connect, using 163.com, 126.com, or yeah.net.

fields.label: Authorization Code
fields.description: The 16-character client authorization code created after enabling IMAP/SMTP in NetEase Mail settings: https://help.mail.163.com/faqDetail.do?code=d7a5dc8471cd0c0e8b4b8f4f8e49998b374173cfe9171305fa1ce630d7f67ac2a5feb28b66796d3b. This is not the NetEase Mail web login password.

actions.name: delete_email
actions.description: Delete one NetEase Mail message from a folder.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for deleting one NetEase Mail email.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that contained the message.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after deleting one NetEase Mail email.

actions.name: download_attachment
actions.description: Download one NetEase Mail attachment by IMAP body part identifier.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

attachmentId.description: The IMAP body part identifier returned in an attachment metadata item.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for downloading one NetEase Mail attachment.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that contained the message.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

attachmentId.description: The IMAP body part identifier returned in an attachment metadata item.

anyOf.description: The attachment size in bytes when available.

downloadUrl.description: The local transit URL for downloading the file.

name.description: The file name used in transit storage.

sizeBytes.description: The stored file size in bytes.

file.description: A file uploaded to local transit file storage.

outputSchema.description: The response returned when downloading one NetEase Mail attachment.

actions.name: forward_email
actions.description: Forward one NetEase Mail email using SMTP with quoted original content.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

text.description: Additional plain text content to prepend before the forwarded message.

html.description: Additional HTML content to prepend before the forwarded message.

filename.description: The filename to use for the outgoing attachment.

contentType.description: The MIME content type of the attachment.

contentUrl.description: The public HTTP or HTTPS URL to fetch attachment content from.

items.description: An attachment to fetch and include in an outgoing email.

attachments.description: Attachments to fetch and include in the outgoing email.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for forwarding one NetEase Mail email.

anyOf.description: The SMTP Message-ID returned by the mail client.

accepted.description: Recipient addresses accepted by the SMTP server.

rejected.description: Recipient addresses rejected by the SMTP server.

response.description: The SMTP response text returned by the server.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after sending a NetEase Mail email.

actions.name: get_email
actions.description: Fetch and parse one NetEase Mail message without marking it as read.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for fetching one NetEase Mail email.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that contained the message.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

anyOf.description: The Message-ID header when available.
The email subject when available.
The display name when one was provided.
The email address when one was provided.
A normalized email address.
The display name when one was provided.
The email address when one was provided.

to.description: The primary recipients parsed from the message.

anyOf.description: The display name when one was provided.
The email address when one was provided.

cc.description: The carbon-copy recipients parsed from the message.

anyOf.description: The message date as an ISO 8601 timestamp.

flags.description: The IMAP flags currently set on the message.

seen.description: Whether the message has the IMAP Seen flag.

anyOf.description: The parsed plain text body within the fetch budget.
The parsed HTML body within the fetch budget.

truncated.description: Whether returned body content was truncated or omitted by budget.

attachmentId.description: The IMAP body part identifier returned in an attachment metadata item.

anyOf.description: The attachment filename when available.
The attachment MIME content type when available.
The attachment size in bytes when available.
The attachment Content-ID when available.

attachments.description: Attachment metadata parsed from the message.

outputSchema.description: The response returned when fetching one NetEase Mail email.

actions.name: get_folder_status
actions.description: Get lightweight message counters for one NetEase Mail folder.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for reading one NetEase Mail folder status.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that was checked.

anyOf.description: The total number of messages when available.
The number of recent messages when available.
The number of unread messages when available.
The next predicted IMAP UID when available.
The mailbox UIDVALIDITY value when available.

outputSchema.description: The response returned when reading one NetEase Mail folder status.

actions.name: list_folders
actions.description: List folders visible to the connected NetEase Mail account.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing NetEase Mail folders.

path.description: The canonical IMAP mailbox path to pass back into folder inputs.

name.description: The decoded display name for the folder.

anyOf.description: The mailbox hierarchy delimiter when available.

flags.description: The IMAP flags attached to this mailbox.

anyOf.description: The special-use mailbox flag when available.

items.description: A NetEase Mail IMAP folder summary.

folders.description: The folders returned by NetEase Mail IMAP.

outputSchema.description: The response returned when listing NetEase Mail folders.

actions.name: mark_email_read
actions.description: Mark one NetEase Mail message as read.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for marking one NetEase Mail email as read.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that contained the message.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

read.description: Whether the message is now marked as read.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after marking one NetEase Mail email as read.

actions.name: mark_email_unread
actions.description: Mark one NetEase Mail message as unread.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for marking one NetEase Mail email as unread.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that contained the message.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

read.description: Whether the message is now marked as read.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after marking one NetEase Mail email as unread.

actions.name: move_email
actions.description: Move one NetEase Mail message to another folder.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for moving one NetEase Mail email.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after moving one NetEase Mail email.

actions.name: reply_email
actions.description: Reply to one NetEase Mail email using SMTP reply headers and quoted content.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

replyAll.description: Whether to include original To and Cc recipients except the connected account.

filename.description: The filename to use for the outgoing attachment.

contentType.description: The MIME content type of the attachment.

contentUrl.description: The public HTTP or HTTPS URL to fetch attachment content from.

items.description: An attachment to fetch and include in an outgoing email.

attachments.description: Attachments to fetch and include in the outgoing email.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for replying to one NetEase Mail email.

anyOf.description: The SMTP Message-ID returned by the mail client.

accepted.description: Recipient addresses accepted by the SMTP server.

rejected.description: Recipient addresses rejected by the SMTP server.

response.description: The SMTP response text returned by the server.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after sending a NetEase Mail email.

actions.name: search_emails
actions.description: Search one NetEase Mail folder and return lightweight email summaries.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path returned by list_folders.

unseen.description: Whether to return only unread messages.

since.description: Only include messages on or after this YYYY-MM-DD date.

before.description: Only include messages before this YYYY-MM-DD date.

limit.description: The maximum number of email summaries to return.

beforeUid.description: Only include message UIDs lower than this cursor.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for searching NetEase Mail emails.

folder.description: The IMAP folder path that was searched.

uid.description: The IMAP UID of the message.

anyOf.description: The Message-ID header when available.
The email subject when available.
The display name when one was provided.
The email address when one was provided.
A normalized email address.
The display name when one was provided.
The email address when one was provided.

to.description: The primary recipients parsed from the message envelope.

anyOf.description: The message date as an ISO 8601 timestamp.

flags.description: The IMAP flags currently set on the message.

seen.description: Whether the message has the IMAP Seen flag.

hasAttachments.description: Whether the message has attachment parts.

anyOf.description: The message size in bytes when available.

items.description: A lightweight NetEase Mail message summary.

emails.description: The email summaries returned for this page.

anyOf.description: The cursor to pass as beforeUid for the next page.

outputSchema.description: The response returned when searching NetEase Mail emails.

actions.name: send_email
actions.description: Send an email through NetEase Mail SMTP.

filename.description: The filename to use for the outgoing attachment.

contentType.description: The MIME content type of the attachment.

contentUrl.description: The public HTTP or HTTPS URL to fetch attachment content from.

items.description: An attachment to fetch and include in an outgoing email.

attachments.description: Attachments to fetch and include in the outgoing email.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for sending an email through NetEase Mail SMTP.

anyOf.description: The SMTP Message-ID returned by the mail client.

accepted.description: Recipient addresses accepted by the SMTP server.

rejected.description: Recipient addresses rejected by the SMTP server.

response.description: The SMTP response text returned by the server.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after sending a NetEase Mail email.
